title: Other health professionals n.e.c.
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 84 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Evaluate hearing and balance disorders to determine diagnoses and treatment plans.
Participate in conferences, training, or publish research to update knowledge on hearing or speech disorder treatments.

Prepare and review reports, manuscripts, or presentations for meetings and publications in professional journals.
Examine physiological, morphological, and cultural characteristics of microorganisms using a microscope to identify and classify them.

Study basic principles of plant and animal life, including classification, development, anatomy, and function.

Consult and advise on medical applications of sciences, such as biology and chemistry, to contribute to public health initiatives.

Conduct research on cell organization and function to improve products or processes in various applications.

Conduct research to develop methodologies and procedures for medical application, analyze data, and present findings.
Determine genetic composition of animal populations and heritability of traits, using principles of genetics.
Crossbreed animals to obtain new combinations of desirable characteristics.
Investigate diseases or parasites to determine cause, risk factors, progress, life cycle, or mode of transmission.

Study soil characteristics to classify soils based on geographic location, landscape position, or properties.
Communicate research or project results to professionals or the public through articles and presentations.
